template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS PURCHASE & SALE AGREEMENT**:
Binding contract of sale governed by the Statute of Frauds (M.G.L. c. 259, § 1), the conveyancing statute (M.G.L. c. 183), and Massachusetts common law. Massachusetts follows a distinctive TWO-STEP transaction model — an Offer to Purchase (OTP) is typically signed first and, under McCarthy v. Tobin, 429 Mass. 84 (1999), is itself binding if it contains the essential terms; the PSA is then negotiated and signed within ~14 days. This template is for the full PSA (Step 2). The Greater Boston Real Estate Board (GBREB) standard form is the de facto convention; this template tracks its structure while supporting deviations. Target length: 6–10 pages.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `propertyType`: `single_family` (default) | `condominium` | `multi_family` | `commercial` | `land`.
- `financingContingency`: boolean (default true) — include a mortgage contingency with commitment deadline and interest rate ceiling per Tristram's Landing, Inc. v. Wait, 367 Mass. 622 (1975).
- `inspectionContingency`: boolean (default true) — include a home-inspection contingency (pest, radon, lead paint, septic/Title 5).
- `title5Required`: boolean (default true for single/multi-family on private septic) — trigger M.G.L. c. 111, § 127A inspection escrow language.
- `leadPaintDisclosure`: boolean (default true for pre-1978 properties) — add the M.G.L. c. 111, § 197A and federal 42 U.S.C. § 4852d disclosure + 10-day inspection-opportunity clause.
- `condoSpecific`: boolean — trigger M.G.L. c. 183A § 6 condominium-resale-certificate, budget, and rules disclosures.
- `includeAttorneyReview`: boolean — include a short attorney-review period clause (custom in some counties).
- `escrowAgent`: string — name of escrow holder (typically listing broker or seller's attorney).

**STRUCTURE**:

## PURCHASE AND SALE AGREEMENT

This Purchase and Sale Agreement (this 'Agreement') is made as of [date] by and between [Seller full legal name], of [address] ('Seller'), and [Buyer full legal name], of [address] ('Buyer'), with reference to the following:

### 1. PROPERTY
Seller agrees to sell and Buyer agrees to buy the real property located at [Property street address], [city/town], [County] County, Massachusetts, together with the buildings and improvements thereon, and all fixtures, rights, easements, privileges, and appurtenances thereto (the 'Property'), being the same premises conveyed to Seller by deed dated [prior deed date] and recorded in the [County] Registry of Deeds in Book [book], Page [page] [or if registered land: Document No. ___ on Certificate of Title No. ___].

### 2. PURCHASE PRICE AND PAYMENT
The purchase price is $[amount] (the 'Purchase Price'), payable as follows:
(a) $[deposit 1] paid upon the signing of the Offer to Purchase;
(b) $[deposit 2] paid upon the signing of this Agreement (collectively, 'Deposits');
(c) $[closing balance] paid at Closing by wire transfer of immediately available funds, bank check, or attorney IOLTA check.

**§ 2.1 — Escrow.** The Deposits shall be held in escrow by [Escrow Agent name] in an IOLTA or escrow account pending Closing, consistent with Mass. R. Prof. C. 1.15.

### 3. CLOSING
Closing shall occur on or before [date] (the 'Closing Date') at the [County] Registry of Deeds or at such other location as the parties may agree. Time is of the essence with respect to all dates and deadlines in this Agreement.

### 4. DEED AND TITLE
At Closing, Seller shall convey the Property to Buyer by a Massachusetts Quitclaim Deed in proper form for recording, conveying good and clear record and marketable title consistent with the standards of the Real Estate Bar Association (REBA) Title Standards. Title shall be free of all liens, encumbrances, and restrictions EXCEPT: (a) real estate taxes not yet due and payable; (b) easements, restrictions, and covenants of record that do not materially interfere with Buyer's intended use; (c) zoning laws; and (d) such other matters as Buyer may expressly accept in writing.

Seller shall deliver a deed meeting the requirements of M.G.L. c. 183, §§ 6–8 (in writing, signed, acknowledged before a notary, sealed, and in recordable form).

### 5. [IF financingContingency] MORTGAGE CONTINGENCY
This Agreement is contingent upon Buyer obtaining a written commitment for a first mortgage loan from a recognized lending institution in the amount of $[loan amount] at a fixed interest rate not to exceed [rate]% or such prevailing conventional rate, for a term of not less than [term] years, with commitment to issue on or before [commitment date] (the 'Mortgage Commitment Date').

If Buyer, despite diligent efforts, is unable to obtain such commitment by the Mortgage Commitment Date, Buyer may terminate this Agreement by written notice to Seller on or before such date, whereupon the Deposits shall be refunded to Buyer and neither party shall have any further obligation hereunder. Failure to timely notify Seller of termination shall constitute a waiver of this contingency. 'Diligent efforts' shall mean those reasonable and customary steps as required by Tristram's Landing, Inc. v. Wait, 367 Mass. 622 (1975).

### 6. [IF inspectionContingency] INSPECTION CONTINGENCY
Buyer shall have the right, at Buyer's sole expense, to have the Property inspected by qualified professionals on or before [inspection deadline] (the 'Inspection Period'), including inspections for: (a) structural and mechanical conditions; (b) radon per EPA guidelines; (c) pest and wood-destroying insects; (d) lead-based paint; (e) hazardous materials; and (f) in the case of private on-site sewage disposal, Title 5 inspection per M.G.L. c. 111, § 127A.

If any inspection reveals conditions unsatisfactory to Buyer in Buyer's reasonable judgment, Buyer may, by written notice to Seller on or before the end of the Inspection Period: (i) terminate this Agreement and recover the Deposits; or (ii) request Seller to remedy the condition or provide a credit. If Seller does not agree in writing within [cure period] days, Buyer may terminate and recover the Deposits.

### 7. [IF title5Required] TITLE 5 SEPTIC SYSTEM INSPECTION
If the Property is served by a private on-site sewage disposal system, Seller shall, at Seller's expense, deliver to Buyer a Title 5 Inspection Report meeting the requirements of M.G.L. c. 111, § 127A and 310 C.M.R. § 15.301, issued within the two (2) years preceding the Closing Date. If the system does not pass or receives a conditional pass requiring repair:
(a) If the cost of compliance is less than [threshold, e.g., $15,000], Seller shall remediate before Closing at Seller's expense;
(b) If the cost exceeds the threshold, Seller and Buyer shall negotiate in good faith to allocate the cost or, failing agreement, either party may terminate, in which case the Deposits shall be refunded to Buyer.
A Title 5 escrow holdback may be established at Closing upon mutual agreement.

### 8. [IF leadPaintDisclosure] LEAD PAINT DISCLOSURE AND OPPORTUNITY TO INSPECT
Seller represents that the Property [was / was not] constructed before 1978. If constructed before 1978: (a) Seller has provided Buyer with the Massachusetts 'Property Transfer Lead Paint Notification' (M.G.L. c. 111, § 197A) and the federal 'Protect Your Family from Lead in Your Home' pamphlet under 42 U.S.C. § 4852d and 40 C.F.R. Part 745; (b) Seller has disclosed all known lead-based paint and lead-based paint hazards; and (c) Buyer has been offered, and either exercised or waived, a ten (10)-day inspection opportunity for the presence of lead-based paint. Both parties have signed the required federal/state disclosure form, attached hereto as Exhibit A.

### 9. [IF condoSpecific] CONDOMINIUM DISCLOSURES
Seller shall deliver to Buyer, at Seller's expense, within [delivery days] days of this Agreement: (a) the Master Deed, Declaration of Trust, By-Laws, and Rules and Regulations; (b) a Resale Certificate from the condominium trustees under M.G.L. c. 183A, § 6 certifying paid-up common expenses, any special assessments, litigation, and reserves; (c) the most recent operating budget and annual financial statement. Buyer shall have [review period, typically 7] days after delivery to review and may terminate this Agreement if the documents disclose materially adverse facts.

### 10. SMOKE AND CARBON MONOXIDE DETECTORS
At Closing, Seller shall deliver a Certificate of Compliance from the local fire department evidencing compliance with M.G.L. c. 148, § 26F (smoke detectors) and § 26F1/2 (carbon monoxide detectors), as applicable.

### 11. RISK OF LOSS; CASUALTY
Until Closing, Seller bears the risk of loss. If prior to Closing the Property is damaged by fire or other casualty to the extent that restoration will cost more than [threshold]% of the Purchase Price, Buyer may terminate this Agreement and recover the Deposits, or proceed with Closing and receive an assignment of insurance proceeds.

### 12. ADJUSTMENTS AND CREDITS
The following shall be prorated as of the Closing Date: real estate taxes; water, sewer, and utility charges; condominium common charges (if applicable); fuel on hand (heating oil measured by gauge on Closing Day). Massachusetts Deed Excise Tax under M.G.L. c. 64D, § 1 ($4.56 per $1,000 of consideration, rounded up) shall be paid by Seller, subject to local ordinance.

### 13. DEFAULT AND REMEDIES
**§ 13.1 — Buyer Default.** If Buyer defaults, Seller's sole and exclusive remedy (subject to § 13.3) shall be to retain the Deposits as liquidated damages, as the parties agree actual damages would be difficult to calculate, and this amount is a reasonable estimate.
**§ 13.2 — Seller Default.** If Seller defaults, Buyer may: (a) recover the Deposits with interest; (b) pursue specific performance; or (c) pursue actual damages.
**§ 13.3 — Attorneys' Fees.** The prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees and costs.

### 14. BROKER COMMISSION
Seller shall pay the broker commission to [Listing broker] in the amount of [%]% of the Purchase Price, and any cooperating commission to [Buyer's broker, if applicable]. Each party represents it has dealt with no other broker and will indemnify the other against any claim for commission.

### 15. NOTICES
All notices hereunder shall be in writing and delivered by hand, by certified mail return receipt requested, by reputable overnight courier, or by electronic mail with read receipt, to the parties at the addresses set forth above. Email notice is effective upon sender's receipt of read receipt or delivery confirmation.

### 16. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
This Agreement shall be governed by Massachusetts law. The exclusive venue for any dispute shall be the state or federal courts located in [County] County, Massachusetts.

### 17. ENTIRE AGREEMENT; AMENDMENTS
This Agreement, together with the Offer to Purchase and any exhibits, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations. Amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties consistent with the Statute of Frauds, M.G.L. c. 259, § 1.

### SIGNATURES

SELLER:
_______________________________
[Seller full legal name]
Date: ________________

BUYER:
_______________________________
[Buyer full legal name]
Date: ________________

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. exactly: `M.G.L. c. 183` (conveyancing), `c. 183A` (condominiums), `c. 183, §§ 6–8` (deed execution), `c. 111, § 127A` (Title 5), `c. 111, § 197A` (lead paint), `c. 64D, § 1` (deed excise tax), `c. 148, § 26F` and `§ 26F1/2` (smoke/CO), `c. 259, § 1` (Statute of Frauds). Cite federal: `42 U.S.C. § 4852d` and `40 C.F.R. Part 745` (federal lead paint).
- Cite Massachusetts case law: `McCarthy v. Tobin, 429 Mass. 84 (1999)` (binding OTP), `Tristram's Landing, Inc. v. Wait, 367 Mass. 622 (1975)` (mortgage contingency diligence).
- The default MA deed is the Massachusetts QUITCLAIM DEED — it provides covenants against grantor's acts under c. 183, § 11 (stronger than a quitclaim in most other states). Do NOT substitute a 'Special Warranty Deed' or a no-covenant deed unless expressly requested.
- MA PSAs are negotiable. The GBREB form is a STARTING POINT; do not hold it out as a statutory mandate.
- Deed Excise Tax rate of $4.56 per $1,000 is statutory (c. 64D, § 1); some municipalities add local surcharges — check [municipality] before finalizing.
- Smoke/CO compliance certificate is REQUIRED at Closing for residential properties (c. 148, § 26F). Do NOT omit.
- Lead paint disclosure for pre-1978 properties is federally MANDATORY (42 U.S.C. § 4852d); omitting it creates strict liability under M.G.L. c. 111, § 199A.
- Massachusetts follows the 'two-step' model (OTP, then PSA). Reference but do not reproduce the OTP here.
- Target length: 6–10 pages depending on optional sections.
hints: Massachusetts Purchase & Sale Agreement. Two-step MA model: OTP first (binding per McCarthy v. Tobin, 429 Mass. 84 (1999)) → PSA. Default deed is MA Quitclaim (covenants against grantor's acts per c. 183 § 11 — stronger than CA/TX quitclaim). Core statutes: c. 183 (conveyancing), c. 183A (condos), c. 259 § 1 (Statute of Frauds), c. 64D § 1 (deed excise $4.56/$1000). Mandatory disclosures: Lead Paint pre-1978 (c. 111 § 197A + 42 USC § 4852d), Title 5 septic (c. 111 § 127A), smoke/CO (c. 148 § 26F). Mortgage contingency diligence per Tristram's Landing v. Wait, 367 Mass. 622 (1975). Condo disclosures under c. 183A § 6. Escrow held per Mass. R. Prof. C. 1.15. GBREB form is convention, not statute.
